articles.title: Oligomerix completes trial of oral tau therapy for Alzheimer’s
articles.summary: Experimental pill targets one of the brain’s most destructive proteins, signaling a broader shift in how biotechs approach cognitive aging. Aging may be inevitable, but losing one’s mind does not have to be. This idea sits beneath one of biotech’s most difficult races: the search for treatments that can slow, delay or even prevent the […] The post Oligomerix completes trial of oral tau therapy for Alzheimer’s appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: China develops national longevity medicine program
articles.summary: New physician training program reflects China’s growing focus on preventive healthspan medicine and clinical geroscience. China has launched its first national, competency-based training program in longevity medicine. It is a massive administrative play to prep the country’s healthcare workforce for a looming demographic crunch. Developed by the China Non-public Medical Institutions Association (CNMIA) and the […] The post China develops national longevity medicine program appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Mitochondrial Aging Linked to Losing Crucial Membrane Lipid
articles.summary: Scientists have found that the levels of phosphatidylcholine, the most abundant lipid in mitochondrial membranes, decline with age, driving mitochondrial […]

articles.title: H-SPAN returns as longevity heads to Washington
articles.summary: Policy, biotech and geroscience converge in DC as longevity science edges further into the American political mainstream. The annual H-SPAN Summit DC, hosted by the Alliance for Longevity Initiatives (A4LI), will return to Georgetown University’s Capitol Campus this summer, bringing together policymakers, regulators, researchers and biotech companies for three days of discussion centered on the […] The post H-SPAN returns as longevity heads to Washington appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Scribe advances safer CRISPR therapies for heart health
articles.summary: Latest preclinical data from ASGCT 2026 suggest the company is pushing gene editing toward safer, longer-lasting preventive medicine. What if gene editing didn’t have to permanently rewrite DNA? What if one treatment could lower cardiovascular risk for years – maybe decades? These questions have hovered over the gene-editing world for years. CRISPR has already transformed […] The post Scribe advances safer CRISPR therapies for heart health appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Codexis debuts sustainable enzymatic RNA manufacturing platform
articles.summary: Codexis presented advances at TIDES USA 2026 in Boston’s Hynes Convention Center, highlighting new data from its ECO Synthesis Manufacturing Platform that, according to the company, demonstrates full-length siRNA synthesis with precise control of phosphorothioate stereochemistry. An expert panel that included drug development leaders from Corsera Health, Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals and Wave Life Sciences discussed the […] The post Codexis debuts sustainable enzymatic RNA manufacturing platform appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Scribe Therapeutics approved for first trial of cholesterol-lowering epigenetic therapy
articles.summary: Scribe Therapeutics has secured clearance from the Australian Therapeutic Goods Administration to initiate a first-in-human clinical study of STX-1150 for the treatment of hypercholesterolemia and reduction of low-density lipoprotein cholesterol. The planned Phase 1 trial is an open-label, single ascending dose study with a subsequent dose expansion phase, designed to assess safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics and […] The post Scribe Therapeutics approved for first trial of cholesterol-lowering epigenetic therapy appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Cognition Therapeutics aligns with FDA on final testing path for Lewy body dementia drug
articles.summary: Cognition Therapeutics completed a planned meeting with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration on 20 May 2026 to review Phase 2 results for Zervimesine (CT1812) and to discuss design and endpoints for a registrational study in dementia with Lewy bodies patients with psychosis. The company says the discussion identified a path forward for development and […] The post Cognition Therapeutics aligns with FDA on final testing path for Lewy body dementia drug appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: BioCardia’s heart cell therapy shows lasting exercise, angina improvements in 2-year study
articles.summary: BioCardia presented preliminary open-label results from the CardiAMP Cell Therapy in Chronic Myocardial Ischemia trial at EuroPCR in Paris on 21 May 2026. According to the company, the cohort experienced no treatment-emergent major adverse cardiac events and showed increased exercise tolerance and reduced angina episodes through two years of follow-up. The company reported exercise tolerance […] The post BioCardia’s heart cell therapy shows lasting exercise, angina improvements in 2-year study appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: How an Oxidative Stress Regulator Makes Cataracts Worse
articles.summary: Researchers have outlined a key receptor and protein involved in the formation of cataracts, paving the way for potential treatments targeting them. Oxidative stress and cataracts are closely related […]

articles.title: 'The Silence of the Lambs' introduced the world to forensic entomology—but how much has changed since?
articles.summary: In the early 1990s, crime-loving television audiences could choose mainly between cozy, fictional detective series such as Columbo and Murder, She Wrote. The US docuseries Unsolved Mysteries brought a few real cold-case investigations to light, but coverage of forensic science on screen was still relatively simple.

articles.title: De‑extinction company says it's made an artificial egg—if true, it could help save living species
articles.summary: Today's announcement by Texas-based de-extinction company Colossal Biosciences about a successful hatching of chicks from an artificial egg would represent a major innovation, if the claims can be verified.

articles.title: New AI system uses cameras and thermal sensors to steer ships clear of gray whales in the San Francisco Bay
articles.summary: Researchers from UC Santa Barbara's Benioff Ocean Science Laboratory (BOSL) and local partners in San Francisco have unveiled new technology in their ongoing efforts to prevent whales and oceangoing vessels from colliding in and near the busy San Francisco Bay.

articles.title: Agentic AI could help electron microscopes plan, adapt and analyze experiments
articles.summary: Scientific discovery is often portrayed as the result of long hours alone in a lab, but true science is inherently collaborative. The most robust experimental processes are developed through partnerships across multiple areas of research.

articles.title: Hi-res microscopes give biologists petabytes of data. Scientists are creating an AI assistant to make sense of it
articles.summary: In a cramped, windowless room on the University of California, Berkeley, campus, two bespoke microscopes—each a Swiss Army knife for high-resolution imaging—operate around the clock gathering data that will help train a game-changing technology for the field of biology: AI.

articles.title: Researchers transform paper sludge into valuable biofuels
articles.summary: Researchers have demonstrated that different types of paper industry sludge, typically treated as a low-value waste, can be transformed into a high-yield renewable biofuel. The study, published in Biofuels, Bioproducts and Biorefining, reveals that paper sludge streams vary significantly in their suitability for producing bioethanol and biogas, offering a pathway to more efficient waste-to-energy strategies.

articles.title: Bacteria found in artisan cheeses may ease disease
articles.summary: Blessed are the tiny cheesemakers: scientists have mapped out the bacteria responsible for giving three British cheeses their distinct flavor, which may also be beneficial to human health. Scientists in the Food Microbial Sciences Unit at the University of Reading, identified the microbial and biochemical profiles of three artisan cheeses made locally in Oxfordshire across their maturation process, and found that the bacteria responsible for a cheese's character could also benefit the people who eat it.

articles.title: AI camera platform to help monitor zoo animals' welfare
articles.summary: An AI-powered camera platform could soon help monitor the health and behavior of zoo animals overnight, thanks to a new partnership between the University of Surrey and Marwell Wildlife. Researchers from Surrey's Centre for Vision, Speech and Signal Processing (CVSSP) are working closely with Marwell to develop a bespoke AI nocturnal behavior observation system that interprets video footage and flags unusual behavioral patterns in animals. The three-year project will use AI and machine learning to study animals' night-time movements, helping zookeepers spot subtle signs of illness or distress that might otherwise go unnoticed.

articles.title: AI-designed miniproteins switch key cell receptors on and off
articles.summary: G protein-coupled receptors, or GPCRs, sit in the plasma membrane, the boundary that defines the inside and outside of a living cell. They communicate with nearly every physiological process in our bodies—from the ability to see and smell, to sensing of adrenaline, insulin, nutrients and medicines.

articles.title: New research offers practical biosecurity tools to limit poultry disease spread
articles.summary: New research from a Texas A&M College of Agriculture and Life Sciences doctoral graduate could help producers better protect poultry flocks from disease outbreaks while reducing costs.

articles.title: Crypto rails are becoming the default payment layer for AI agents, report says
articles.summary: A new report from Keyrock says stablecoins on blockchain rails are becoming the go-to payment layer for AI agents as traditional card rails struggle to handle micropayments.

articles.title: CFTC officials who questioned prediction markets were suspended: NYT
articles.summary: A New York Times investigation found that senior CFTC officials who raised concerns about Polymarket, Crypto.com and Gemini were suspended and pushed out.

articles.title: Blockchain researcher defends Ethereum Foundation, says it’s ‘exactly’ doing its job
articles.summary: William Mougayar says critics are measuring the Ethereum Foundation by the wrong standard, claiming it was never meant to pump ETH or court institutions.

articles.title: Why is Bitcoin falling despite pro-crypto Kevin Warsh becoming Fed chair?
articles.summary: Rising short-term bond yields and Warsh's hawkish comments in the past are reviving fears of a December rate hike, which could slam the brakes on Bitcoin's recovery.

articles.title: Euro and USD stablecoins depeg amid ongoing $2.8M StablR exploit
articles.summary: The suspected cause is a private key compromise of one owner in the minting multisig account, said Blockaid.

articles.title: 70% of all crypto wrench attacks happen in France: Report
articles.summary: Centralized data collection is a honeypot for hackers and organized criminals looking to target crypto holders and their families, according to Bitcoiners.

articles.title: Bitcoin heads higher as President Trump announces Iran peace agreement
articles.summary: "An Agreement has been largely negotiated, subject to finalization between the United States of America, the Islamic Republic of Iran, and the various other Countries," wrote President Trump late Saturday afternoon.

articles.title: Analysts forecast a Bitcoin crash to the $60K level, revisiting 2026 low
articles.summary: The price of Bitcoin is about $75,800 at the time of publication, a nearly 40% decrease from the all-time high of about $126,000 reached in October 2025.

articles.title: ECB warns EU finance ministers that easing euro stablecoin rules would weaken banks: Reuters
articles.summary: ECB President Christine Lagarde warned the changes would destabilize bank funding and weaken interest-rate transmission.

articles.title: Bitcoin is ready to beat stocks and bonds again after underperformance against Wall Street
articles.summary: Former Credit Suisse global head of portfolio and Risk Dimensions CIO Mark Connors says bitcoin has broken out of its longest stretch of underperformance in history and is ready to beat stocks, bonds, and gold as inflation stubbornly sticks around.
